texto: People bear loneliness every day. They think they won't be able to, that they won't survive, but somehow one second slips into another, becomes an hour, a day, a week, and they are still living. They are still alone, even in the middle of a crowd of people. Even with a partner, a child.
explicacao: Citação de Laura Barnett sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “People bear loneliness every day. They think they won't be able to, that they won't survive, but …” articula reflexao, loneliness, people em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

They are still alone, even in the middle of a crowd of people. Even with a partner, a child.

texto: You walked into my life when I was nineteen years old. You were the only man I ever loved - the only man I ever hope to love. You took everything we did together, everything we were to each other, and scorched it to nothing: left it a cloud of ash.
explicacao: Citação de Laura Barnett sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “You walked into my life when I was nineteen years old. You were the only man I ever loved - the o…” articula amor, heartbreak, love em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como love e young-love.

texto: That's what marriage is, isn't it? Taking the rough with the smooth. At least, that's what it should be.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Laura Barnett que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “That's what marriage is, isn't it? Taking the rough with the smooth. At least, that's what it sho…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, marriage, taking, com foco em casamento e reflexao.
